description: Golden-query regression harness. 20 queries total: 5 Saturday anchors + 8 from sir's verified 10-query set + 7 new edges (2 expected-fails that showcase Phase 3's future capabilities).

buckets.anchor: The five Saturday stress-test queries. Must stay green across every Phase 3/4/etc. change.
buckets.sir: From sir's verified 10-query set in 00_README_and_Schema.xlsx. Ground truth manually computed by sir.
buckets.edge: New stretch tests. Two are expected-fail today (marked expected_current='fail') to measure Phase 3 improvements.

queries.label: Critical services + behind-training + flagged vendors
queries.query: Show me critical services where the owning team is behind on mandatory training and uses any flagged vendors. Include the training gap and the vendor names.
queries.require_pass_rationale: Measured ~76-85% true pass rate across 33 samples (Q1_x6.json, Q1_x6_post_B.json, Q1_x6_flag_OFF.json, Q1_x6_flag_ON_B.json, and 3×20 runs). LLM non-determinism on the aggregate-row assertion (sometimes emits rows=0 or 154 instead of 22). With --runs 3, require_pass=2 matches the binomial expectation; earlier 3/3 readings in pre_phase3.json / post_phase3_z.json were lucky samples, not stable-pass. See findings.md 'B — SHIPPED' section.
queries.source: SOLUTIONS §1

queries.label: Retention + on-call exposure for serious-incident engineers
queries.query: What's our exposure if we wanted to lock in the engineers who handled all our serious incidents last year? Include both retention bonuses and what we paid them for being on-call.
queries.source: SOLUTIONS §2

queries.label: IPO-risk Asian customers w/o ESOPs + no certs
queries.query: We're heading into IPO. Which of our biggest accounts in regulated Asian markets are being managed by people who don't have ESOPs and haven't bothered with any certifications?
queries.source: SOLUTIONS §3

queries.label: Laptop spend vs Engineering Q4 budget
queries.query: Does our actual laptop spend on senior engineering staff line up with what's in Engineering's reported Q4 budget? Where's the gap, if any?
queries.source: SOLUTIONS §4

queries.label: Data-localization revenue + AI infra readiness
queries.query: We're scaling AI investment heavily next year — how much revenue do we have sitting in markets where data localization could blow up on us, and is our AI infrastructure even set up to handle that?
queries.source: SOLUTIONS §5

queries.label: Tier-1 APAC customers + AM has not completed DPDP training
queries.query: Which Tier-1 APAC customers are managed by account managers who haven't completed DPDP Act 2023 training?
queries.source: Sir's README Q1 — ground truth: Crescent Media + Ascend Logistics (both Rohit Jain)

queries.label: SEV-1/SEV-2 incidents in AI Research + reporter CTC
queries.query: Show me all SEV-1 and SEV-2 incidents reported by employees in the Data & AI Research department, with each reporter's name and total CTC.
queries.source: Sir's README Q3 — ground truth: 5 incidents across those reporters

queries.label: L5+ with Apple laptops AND unresolved training
queries.query: Which L5+ employees have Apple laptops assigned AND have unresolved mandatory training?
queries.source: Sir's README Q4 — ground truth: 6 employees

queries.label: Departments ranked by remediation_cost / quarterly_budget
queries.query: Rank departments by the ratio of total remediation cost to their quarterly budget.
queries.source: Sir's README Q5 — ground truth: Engineering ~14.32

queries.label: Flagged vendors linked to assets
queries.query: Show me vendors with Conditional or Suspended risk status that are linked to any assets.
queries.source: Sir's README Q6 — ground truth: NVIDIA (DGX), VendorConnect Solutions

queries.label: Top 10 customers by ARR + AM + manager's manager
queries.query: Give me the top 10 customers by ARR including their account manager's name and the name of their account manager's manager.
queries.source: Sir's README Q7 — ground truth: 10 rows with self-join manager chain

queries.label: Critical services with >2 incidents
queries.query: Which critical services have had more than two incidents? Include the owning department.
queries.source: Sir's README Q9 — ground truth: data-svc-26 (Engineering)

queries.label: Vendor spend per department, by vendor category
queries.query: How much did each department spend on each vendor category last fiscal year?
queries.source: Sir's README Q2 — ground truth: Engineering/Cloud ~228 Cr (category/period specifics vary). Use as structural test only.

queries.label: NOT condition — departments without critical services
queries.query: Which departments don't have any services classified as Critical in the products catalog?

expected.notes: Engineering owns the vast majority of critical services, so it MUST NOT appear. Other departments may or may not — anti-join should produce a small set.

queries.source: New edge — tests anti-join pattern (Pattern E)

queries.label: Self-join — managers with biggest teams
queries.query: Which managers have the largest direct-report headcount? Show the top 5 with their team size.
queries.source: New edge — tests self-join on manager_employee_id + groupby aggregation

queries.label: 4-way chain — ESOP value for L5+ in flagged-vendor departments
queries.query: What's the total ESOP value at INR 842/share FMV for all L5+ employees whose department owns any flagged vendor?

scalar_checks.name: total_esop_value_inr
scalar_checks.notes: Very loose tolerance — structural test; exact value requires pulling sir's ground-truth separately

queries.source: New edge — tests salary_records × employees × departments × vendors

queries.label: Worst compliance department — aggregate vs per-module
queries.query: Which department has the worst mandatory-training compliance, and what's their completion rate?

expected.notes: Engineering's aggregate rate is ~87% (below 90%). Other departments may also be below. Structural test — any Engineering + rate < 0.90 counts as pass.

queries.source: New edge — probes Pattern H (total-over-total rate, not mean-of-means)

queries.label: Best ARR-to-CTC account manager
queries.query: Who is the best account manager in terms of ARR managed divided by their own total CTC?

expected.notes: Ground truth: Rohit Jain, ratio ~258.38 (ARR 5834.29 / CTC 22.58). Tests Pattern G: aggregate-then-rank.

queries.source: Verified post-fix query from sir's additional tests

queries.label: Expected-fail — derived metric: revenue per engineer
queries.query: What's the revenue per engineer in each department? Rank departments by this metric.

scalar_checks.name: engineering_revenue_per_engineer_inr_lakhs
scalar_checks.notes: Engineering owns ~all customers (all AMs are in Sales, but 'engineers' = technical staff). The SOLUTIONS-equivalent derivation: sum customers.arr_inr_lakhs / count of dept tech headcount. Ground-truth estimate: total ARR ~16000 L / ~90 tech staff ≈ 180 L/engineer. Loose tolerance.

expected.notes: Pass requires the derived metric to be computed AND Engineering to be identified as a technical dept. Current one-shot code-gen typically: (a) uses financial_transactions.amount as revenue (wrong — that's spend, not revenue), (b) counts all employees not just engineers, (c) lumps Sales AMs as 'engineers'. Phase 3 planner should decompose: 'numerator = sum customers.arr where AM dept is in tech depts; denominator = count employees in tech depts'.

queries.source: New edge — EXPECTED-FAIL today, measures Phase 3 derived-metric capability

queries.label: Expected-fail — temporal filter with no direct column
queries.query: Show me revenue churn in the last 90 days. Which customers reduced their licensed seats?

expected.notes: Pass requires the system to RECOGNIZE that the corpus has no time-series of licensed_seats — only a current snapshot. Expected answer: an honest 'cannot compute — corpus has point-in-time data only'. Current one-shot code-gen either hallucinates a date column (fails silently with 0 rows) or invents a fake proxy. Phase 3 planner must DETECT missing data dimension at the plan stage and surface it instead of proceeding to broken code.

queries.source: New edge — EXPECTED-FAIL today, measures Phase 3 'recognize missing data dimension' capability
